title: Siderodendron ferreum
taxonomy: Siderodendron ferreum is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Santalaceae. It belongs to the genus Siderodendron, which is a group of plants commonly known as "iron trees" due to their ability to tolerate high levels of iron in the soil. This plant is endemic to the tropical regions of Central and South America. Its classification is based on its morphological characteristics, such as its unique leaf structure and woody growth habit. Siderodendron ferreum is considered a rare and endangered species due to habitat loss and degradation.

intro: Siderodendron ferreum, also known as iron tree, is a tropical tree native to the Pacific island of Guam. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, and has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues.
